ops.title: Guard-pack bounded lanes: sed/awk sinks + capped dir walk — accepted residuals
ops.body:
The grep/rg flood guard gained two allow-lanes (user report: `grep -riE "buck2|reindeer" aqua.yaml aqua/ … | sed -n '1,20p'` blocked): terminal `sed`/`awk` head-equivalents as bounding sinks (`sed_bounded`/`awk_bounded`/`sed_addr_bounded` in search_common.py, structural parsers, no regex), and a size-capped directory walk (`tree_size_capped`, 20KB `LARGE_READ_BYTES` + 2000-entry cap) admitting dir operands to the stat lanes — plain `-r` only for grep (`-R` dereferences symlinks the walk skips); rg forfeits on `-L`.

Review trail: sol finder (3 confirmed bugs: rg data-ext lane admitted directories named like data files; `stat(follow_symlinks=False)` on operands regressed symlinked files — grep/rg dereference command-line paths; `isdigit()`→`int()` crashes on `²` and >4300-digit runs, fixed via `plain_count`) + a clinically-reframed sol refuter (first attempt died to OpenAI's cybersecurity filter on "break it" phrasing — reframe as "boundedness verification audit"). 12 of 13 refuter findings fixed fail-closed: BSD sed `0q` (address 0 never matches → autoprint), flags after the sed script positional (BSD reads them as files), awk embedded-newline rule separation, `--color=always` SGR amplification (grep + rg), rg `--replace`/`-r` multiplication, rg `-z` on compressed *files* (not just dirs), `export GREP_OPTIONS`/`RIPGREP_CONFIG_PATH` earlier in the line (raw-line screen in grep_bounded/rg_bounded; the sink lane deliberately survives — terminals cap output regardless of injected flags), brace operands (`x.{log,py}` breaks suffix judgment; `has_magic` misses braces), expansion operands statting crafted literals (stat/walk lanes only), and `>&2`//dev/std* sink bypasses (`writes_terminal_device` now scans each stage's raw, source included; `2>&1` stays exempt — it merges INTO the pipe).

**Accepted residuals — do not "fix" without re-reading this:** (1) filename-prefix amplification: the walk bounds match content, not per-line `path:line` prefixes — a dense-match tree of long paths amplifies, same class as the data-ext lane's long-line exposure; (2) the no-stat data-ext suffix lane still admits `~`/`$`-expansion operands (`grep foo ~/app.log`, `rg foo $d/app.log` are PINNED Allow rows — the suffix survives tilde/var prefixes, and screening them broke those deliberate escapes; only braces forfeit that lane); (3) hooks run from the installed plugin snapshot (~/.claude/plugins/cache/cc-context/…), so live sessions see the new lanes only after the next plugin release.
